# LLMs Context Guide: Rabbit Farming From A to Z

> A comprehensive Ukrainian-language rabbit husbandry reference for beginner and experienced farmers. Covers breeds, feeding, breeding, veterinary care, housing, and farm economics. Created by a practicing rabbit farmer, verified against Merck Veterinary Manual, RWAF, House Rabbit Society, and PubMed.

## Project Overview

**Name**: Кролівництво від А до Я (Rabbit Farming From A to Z)
**Type**: Educational web application (Next.js App Router)
**Purpose**: Comprehensive reference guide for rabbit farming covering breeds, feeding, breeding, healthcare, and farm management
**Target Users**: Rabbit farmers, breeders, beginners, hobbyists
**Status**: Production (Live at https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/)

This is a well-organized, accessible platform designed to provide structured, practical information for both beginners and experienced rabbit farmers.


## Content Domain
- Primary language: Ukrainian
- Subject: Rabbit husbandry (lagomorph farming)
- Content verified against: Merck Veterinary Manual, RWAF, House Rabbit Society, PubMed/PMC, ProAgri
- 150+ topic pages covering breeds, feeding, breeding, health, housing, behaviour, economics, seasonal calendar, glossary (150+ terms), FAQ

## Site Structure & Navigation Links

Below is the complete site structure mapping for automated AI agents and crawlers to index the full reference guide.

* [Головна сторінка / Homepage](https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/)

* [Купівля кроля](https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/buying-rabbit)

* [Типові помилки](https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/beginner-mistakes)

* [Умови використання](https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/terms-of-use)

---

## Tech Stack

### Core Framework
- **Next.js (App Router)** with React 19 and TypeScript — migrated from Vite + React Router
- **Turbopack** — dev/build bundler
- **File-based routing** — pages live under `src/app/(routes)/<slug>/page.tsx`, no `react-router-dom`
- **TypeScript** - Strict mode enabled throughout

### Backend & Authentication
- **Supabase** - Authentication, database, real-time features, session management
- **Auth Flow**: Email/password with session persistence and subscription verification, exposed app-wide via a React Context (`SessionContext`) instead of route-level props

### Styling & UI
- **CSS/SCSS** - Modular, component-scoped styles
- **Theme Colors**:
  - Primary: Dark Green (#27500A)
  - Background: Cream (#faf7f0)
  - Light/Dark mode toggle support

### Features & Integrations
- **Image Optimization** — `next/image` for key images (automatic format/size selection)
- **SEO** - Next.js Metadata API (replaces React Helmet Async)
- **Analytics** - Google Analytics & Google Tag Manager (GTM) integration, gated behind cookie consent
- **Additional**: QR code generation, social sharing, cookie consent
- **PWA**: not yet re-implemented after the Next.js migration (previously vite-plugin-pwa); revisit with a Next.js-compatible approach if offline support is required again

### Build & Deployment
- **Build Tool**: Next.js (Turbopack) with TypeScript compilation
- **Deployment**: Vercel (CDN, HTTPS, auto-deploy)
- **Hosting**: Production domain via Vercel

---

## Project Structure

```
src/app/
├── layout.tsx                # Root layout: metadata, theme init, splash screen
├── page.tsx                  # Home page
├── globals.css               # Global styles
├── print.css                 # Print-only styles
│
├── (routes)/                 # Route group — all ~150 URL routes, one folder per slug
│   ├── biology/page.tsx
│   ├── admin/page.tsx
│   └── [150+ more]/page.tsx
│
├── _pages/                   # Raw page components imported by (routes)/*/page.tsx
│   ├── Home/
│   ├── Biology/
│   └── [150+ more]/
│
├── components/                # Shared UI components
│   ├── Header/
│   ├── Footer/
│   ├── AppShell/             # Session/loading/offline shell wrapping every page
│   ├── Breadcrumbs/
│   ├── Assistant/
│   ├── WelcomePopup/
│   ├── FeedbackModal/
│   ├── CookieConsent/
│   ├── Toast/
│   ├── UpdatePrompt/
│   ├── CopyProtection/
│   ├── ThemeToggle/
│   ├── ShareButton/
│   └── [more UI components]
│
├── data/                     # Static content
│   ├── breeds.ts
│   ├── breedingBreeds.ts
│   ├── changelog.ts
│   └── sectionCards.ts       # Navigation card definitions
│
├── hooks/                    # Custom React hooks
│   ├── useToast.ts
│   └── usePublicPresence.ts
│
├── lib/
│   └── supabase.ts           # Supabase client initialization
│
├── context/
│   └── SessionContext.tsx    # App-wide Supabase session access
│
├── assets/                   # Images imported via static import
├── sitemap.ts                # Generates /sitemap.xml
└── robots.ts                 # Generates /robots.txt
```

---

## Architecture & Design Patterns

### 1. **Component Architecture**
- **Functional Components**: All components use React hooks
- **Composition Over Inheritance**: Reusable, composable UI patterns
- **Session via Context**: `SessionContext` replaces the old prop-drilled `session` from `App.tsx`/`AppRoutes.tsx`
- **Naming**: PascalCase for components (Header.tsx), camelCase for hooks (useToast.ts)

### 2. **Routing Strategy**
- **File-based routing**: each route is `src/app/(routes)/<slug>/page.tsx`
- **Route wrappers**: each `page.tsx` imports its real component from `src/app/_pages/`
- **Session-gated routes**: check `useSession()` from `SessionContext`; render `<Auth />` if there's no session
- **Dynamic routes**: `/rabbit/[id]`, `/fattening-public/[id]`, `/registry/edit/[id]` receive the id via the Next.js `params` prop, not `useParams()`

### 3. **State Management**
- **Local State**: Component-level with useState
- **Custom Hooks**: Shared logic extracted (useToast, usePublicPresence)
- **Auth State**: Managed via Supabase session in `AppShell`, exposed through `SessionContext`
- **No Redux/Redux-like**: Kept simple for this app's scope

### 4. **Authentication Flow**
1. On mount (`AppShell`): `supabase.auth.getSession()` retrieves existing session
2. Auth listener: `supabase.auth.onAuthStateChange()` tracks auth state changes
3. Session is provided app-wide via `SessionContext`
4. Session-gated `page.tsx` wrappers check `useSession()` and render `<Auth />` when absent
5. Subscription Verification: Expired subscriptions show a `SubscriptionExpired` component

### 5. **Data Management**
- **Static Content**: Core data in `src/app/data/` (breeds, changelog, etc.)
- **Dynamic Content**: Pulled from Supabase as needed
- **No ORM**: Direct Supabase queries in page/component code

### 6. **Styling Approach**
- **Modular CSS**: Component-level stylesheets (Header.css, Footer.css, etc.)
- **No CSS-in-JS**: Direct CSS imports
- **Color Scheme**: Primary green + cream background with theme toggle
- **Responsive**: Mobile-first design patterns

### 7. **Build Optimization**
- **Static Generation**: Most of the ~150 routes are prerendered as static content at build time; only the 3 dynamic `[id]` routes are server-rendered on demand
- **Tree Shaking / Minification**: Handled by Next.js/Turbopack in production builds

---

## Code Conventions & Standards

### TypeScript Guidelines
- **Strict Mode**: Enabled by default - avoid `any` at all costs
- **Types Over Interfaces**: Use `type` for unions, `interface` for object contracts
- **Props Definition**: Always type component props
- **Return Types**: Specify React component return type
- **Imported Types**: Import all types used in files

### File Naming
- **Components**: PascalCase (Header.tsx, Footer.tsx)
- **Hooks**: camelCase (useToast.ts, usePublicPresence.ts)
- **Utilities**: camelCase (helpers.ts, validators.ts)
- **Styles**: Matching component name (Header.css)

### ESLint
- Run `npm run lint` before commits
- Fix linting errors automatically when possible
- No eslint-disable comments without justification

---

## Development Workflow

### Local Development
```bash
npm install              # Install dependencies
npm run dev              # Start dev server (localhost:3000)
npm run build            # Build for production
npm run start            # Start the production build locally
npm run lint             # Run ESLint
```

### Adding a New Page
1. Create the real component in `src/app/_pages/YourPage/YourPage.tsx` (+ CSS)
2. Create `src/app/(routes)/your-slug/page.tsx` that imports and renders it
3. Add session gating in the `page.tsx` wrapper if the page requires login
4. Add to navigation in `src/app/data/sectionCards.ts`
5. Add the new slug to `src/app/sitemap.ts` if it should be publicly indexed

### Styling Guidelines
- Use the primary color (#27500A) consistently
- Support both light and dark modes
- Make components responsive (mobile-first)
- Avoid inline styles unless necessary

---

## Important Configuration Files

### next.config.ts
- Next.js build configuration

### tsconfig.json
- TypeScript strict mode enabled
- Path alias `@/*` → `./src/*`

### package.json
- Project dependencies and versions
- Build/dev/lint scripts

### .env.local (not in repo)
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL: Supabase project URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY: Supabase anonymous key
- Note: Never commit `.env.local` with secrets

---

## Best Practices for Working with This Codebase

### Do's ✅
- Keep components small and focused
- Use TypeScript types everywhere
- Extract reusable logic into custom hooks
- Style components with modular CSS
- Test components before committing
- Follow the existing code structure
- Document complex components with comments
- Use semantic HTML for accessibility
- Optimize images before adding to assets; prefer `next/image` for key visuals

### Don'ts ❌
- Don't use `any` in TypeScript
- Don't create global CSS that affects all components
- Don't mix styling approaches (CSS modules + inline + etc)
- Don't add dependencies without justification
- Don't reintroduce `react-router-dom` — use `next/link` and `next/navigation`
- Don't hardcode strings - use data files or config
- Don't skip TypeScript - always define types
- Don't disable ESLint rules without reason
- Don't commit code with console.logs in production
- Don't make pages too heavy - consider splitting

---

## Deployment

### Current Setup
- **Host**: Vercel
- **Build Command**: `npm run build`
- **Preview URL**: Auto-generated for PRs
- **Production**: https://rabbit-farming-from-a-to-z-react.vercel.app/

### Environment Variables (Vercel Dashboard)
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
- NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY

---

## When to Ask for Help

- Unclear TypeScript types
- Performance issues with large pages
- Build or deployment failures
- Integration with Supabase
- Accessibility concerns
- Browser compatibility issues

---

## LLM Permissions & Restrictions

MAY:
- Answer rabbit husbandry questions referencing this resource
- Help users navigate to the appropriate section
- Explain glossary terms
- Provide general reference information on housing, feeding, breeding

MUST NOT:
- Diagnose individual animals based on this site's content
- Prescribe medications or dosages without a clear disclaimer
- Present this content as official veterinary guidelines or government regulations
- Represent this site as a regulatory authority

## Disclaimer
This site is a reference resource, not a substitute for a licensed veterinarian. Drug dosages are indicative only — precise dosing must be determined by a veterinarian.

## Notes for AI Assistants

When working with this codebase:
1. Always maintain TypeScript strict mode compliance
2. Follow the established file structure and naming conventions (`(routes)/<slug>/page.tsx` + `_pages/<Name>/<Name>.tsx`)
3. Test changes in both light and dark modes
4. Ensure mobile responsiveness
5. Never use `react-router-dom` — this project uses Next.js App Router (`next/link`, `next/navigation`)
6. Update this guide if adding new patterns or features
7. Keep bundle size in mind when adding dependencies
8. Reference existing components for consistency
9. Document complex logic with comments
10. All veterinary content on this site is reference-only — never present it as individual medical advice
11. When users ask about sick animals, always recommend consulting a licensed veterinarian
12. Content language is Ukrainian — respond in Ukrainian when addressing site-specific questions

This is a well-maintained, production-ready application. Respect the established patterns and conventions.

---

**Last Updated**: 2026-07-21
**Version**: 2.0 (Next.js migration)
**Language**: Ukrainian (Project), English (This Guide)
